prompt:

Write the voice-over as a continuous, chronological story.

Rules:
- Follow the timestamps strictly. Never describe a later moment before an earlier one.
- Ground every claim in the digest. If the digest does not say a train is present, do not put one there.
- Name stations only when the on-screen text names them.
- Vary the language; do not repeat the same sentence shape.
- Write one section per title card, in order. Begin each section with a markdown heading naming that line exactly, e.g. '## Bakerloo Line'. Mention the timestamp of at least one moment in each section as m:ss.
- No preamble, no closing commentary. Begin with the first heading.
response:
## Bakerloo Line
At 0:09, we begin our journey at Embankment, where the iconic red and blue roundel marks the station wall. On the platform, a train sits with its doors sealed, while a small group of commuters, including a woman in the foreground, wait in the quiet anticipation of the underground. By 1:08, the crowd has dispersed, leaving the platform empty save for a promotional poster for Pete Tong clinging to the wall.

## Central Line
Moving to the Central Line at 2:21, we find ourselves at Bethnal Green. The scene opens on a close-up of the station signage before widening to show a train with its doors open, inviting passengers aboard. A few travelers stroll down the platform, passing a series of vibrant advertisements that line the tiled walls.

## Circle Line
At 4:41, the view shifts to Temple. A train has come to a halt here, though the platform remains deserted. Through the closed glass of the carriages, we can see the silhouettes of passengers waiting for the signal to depart.

## District Line
By 5:52, we arrive at Stepney Green. The white brick walls provide a backdrop for the station sign as a train pulls in. Unlike the previous stop, this platform is active, with about seven people milling about—some pacing the length of the station, others standing in still reflection.

## Hammersmith City Line
Returning to Stepney Green at 8:57, we see a different perspective as a train opens its doors, allowing a passenger to step off into the station while others remain seated inside the carriage. The journey then takes us to Swiss Cottage at 9:00, where the camera lingers on the station sign against the white tiles.

## Jubilee Line
At 10:13, we witness another arrival with doors open and passengers disembarking. By 10:16, the atmosphere at Swiss Cottage changes; a train appears as a blur of motion, its doors shut tight. A lone figure stands far down the platform, dwarfed by the "Way Out" signage.

## Metropolitan Line
At 12:26, we reach Finchley Road. This segment takes us above ground to an outdoor platform where the Northbound line is in full swing. Between 12:30 and 14:56, a bustling crowd of passengers boards the open-doored train, their movements captured against the open air of the Metropolitan line.

## Northern Line
At 15:00, we descend once more to Belsize Park. A train waits with closed doors while a digital display counts down three minutes until the arrival of the Charing Cross service. Passengers are visible both on the platform and peering through the train windows. By 17:01, the train has departed, leaving two people to walk alone along the empty platform.

## Piccadilly Line
The scene shifts to Finsbury Park at 17:15, starting with a stark shot of the station sign. At 17:19, a train sits stationary with its doors closed. A man in a black Nike tracksuit stands on the platform, positioned beneath signs directing travelers toward the National Rail connection.

## Victoria Line
At 19:47, we encounter the signage for Blackhorse Road. The following view shows a train with closed doors and a solitary figure walking in the distance, while the walls are adorned with various advertising posters.

## Waterloo City Line
Our final stop arrives at 21:44 at Waterloo. After a brief look at the station sign, we see a train stopped with its doors closed. The platform is entirely vacant, leaving only a yellow "Danger high voltage" warning sign to guard the metal railings.
